questions.prompt: Which renderer path fixes to include?
questions.description: 10 sites across App.tsx, FileGrid.tsx, and FolderTree.tsx use split('/') without backslash normalization, breaking on Windows.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Fix all 10 sites plus create a shared utility, then use it everywhere including MetadataEditor.tsx and ExtraTagsEditor.tsx (which already normalize inline).

options.label: Fix ALL 10 sites + create shared util + normalize existing inline patterns
Fix only the 5 critical sites (dirPath, album detection, cover art, undo snapshots)

questions.prompt: Investigate the cancel emission timing gap?
questions.description: cancelTask() emits via updateTask() while failCancelled() emits via signal abort. Between the two, there's a window where no 'cancelled' event fires until the blocking operation finishes or the renderer polls again.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Investigate and fix if confirmed — the renderer polls every 200ms so user impact is low, but a real fix adds an immediate emit to cancelTask().

options.label: Add a code comment noting the gap, no behavioral change
Skip — the polling fallback covers it

questions.description: state.undoManager.push() mutates the existing UndoManager object in place rather than producing a new reference.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Fix by adding a cloneAndPush() method to UndoManager and using it in the reducer.

options.label: Fix — add cloneAndPush() and use it in the reducer
Skip — it works and only affects internal UndoManager state

questions.description: The beforeEach/afterEach env isolation is duplicated in two describe blocks (~15 lines each).
questions.recommendedAnswer: Extract into a shared helper function (setupEnv / teardownEnv) used by both describe blocks.

options.label: Skip — duplication is acceptable for test clarity

questions.prompt: Add tests for the new path utility?
questions.description: A shared normalizePath/toPosix utility should have its own unit tests covering both POSIX and Windows paths.

options.label: Skip — existing tests implicitly cover it
